figures.caption: Figure 4: Real-world setup and qualitative results. Left: the robotic platform, including a Franka arm equipped with wristmounted and external RGB-D cameras. Right: example task executions in real-world and the SimplerEnv benchmark.

Table 1: Quantitative comparison on the SimplerEnv-WidowX benchmark [23] using OpenVLA [17] as the baseline. † indicates reproduced performance. We report the average success rate (%) over 50 trials on 4 tasks.
Table 2: Quantitative comparison on the LIBERO benchmark. Success rates (%) are averaged over 50 trials per task across four task suites, each containing 10 tasks.
Figure 8: Visualization results of task-relevant objects grounding.
Figure 7: Visualization results of depth estimation.
Table 5: Ablation study on SimplerEnv and real-world tasks.

Figure 2: Overview of VeriSpace. At test time, a frozen VLA policy first samples multiple candidate actions from the current observation and instruction. VeriSpace then evaluates each candidate through two key components: a 3D-aware scene encoding module that injects explicit scene geometry into the visual representation, and a spatial reasoning module that assesses each action under the current scene and task goal. The resulting verification scores are used to rank the sampled actions and select the final action for execution.
Figure 13: Qualitative results of our VeriSpace on the SimplerEnv-WidowX benchmark [23]. Representative examples include Eggplant In Basket, Stack Cube, Carrot On Plate and Spoon On Tower tasks.
Figure 11: The scene setting of the real-world experiments.
Figure 9: Examples of CoT data annotation.
Figure 10: Prompt template for candidate action scoring.
Figure 6: Our pipeline for generating synthetic embodied chain-of-thought data.

Figure 12: Qualitative results of our VeriSpace on real-world tasks. Representative examples include Tomato on Plate, Cube on Plate, Stack Cubes, Pepper on Plate, Cube in Drawer and Banana on Plate tasks.
